content.text: The {{c1::best}} AI product starts with a use case that has clear value, clear constraints, and enough data to support iteration.
content.explanation: Good AI products are not defined by the model alone, but by the business problem they solve.
content.spanish: El mejor producto de IA comienza con un caso de uso que tenga claro valor, límites definidos y datos suficientes para iterar.
content.usage: Useful for product managers, founders, and AI strategy teams.

content.text: A strong {{c1::ROI}} argument for AI combines cost savings, time savings, quality gains, and risk reduction in a realistic estimate.
content.explanation: Executive stakeholders often need a simple, credible business case rather than a purely technical pitch.
content.spanish: Un argumento sólido de ROI para IA combina ahorros de costo, tiempo, mejora de calidad y reducción de riesgo en una estimación realista.
content.usage: Very relevant for leaders discussing AI investment and adoption.

content.text: {{c1::Adoption}} fails when users are not trained, supported, and given a clear reason to change their habits.
content.explanation: Technical excellence is not enough if the human side of change is ignored.
content.spanish: La adopción fracasa cuando los usuarios no reciben capacitación, soporte y una razón clara para cambiar sus hábitos.
content.usage: Central for internal copilots, automation tools, and AI-assisted workflows.

content.text: The {{c1::best}} AI leaders explain trade-offs in plain language and connect the technology to business outcomes.
content.explanation: Communication is part of product strategy because AI systems often create uncertainty for non-technical stakeholders.
content.spanish: Los mejores líderes de IA explican los compromisos en un lenguaje sencillo y conectan la tecnología con resultados de negocio.
content.usage: Essential for product leads and managers working across functions.
